metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: The glass slipped from Maria's hand. The glass fell to the floor. The glass broke into many pieces. Maria swept the floor carefully.

Dark clouds gathered over the garden in the late afternoon. Heavy rain fell on the garden for nearly an hour. The laundry became completely soaked on the washing line. Tomás carried the laundry into the warm kitchen.
Elena forgot the bread in the oven for too long. The bread turned black around the crust. Thick smoke filled the kitchen within a few minutes. Elena opened every window in the kitchen.
A strong wind pushed against the old oak tree. The old oak tree cracked near the base of the trunk. The old oak tree fell across the narrow road. Workers cut the old oak tree into short logs.
The cat knocked the vase off the wooden shelf. The vase shattered on the tile floor below. Water from the vase spread across the tile floor. Jonas wiped the tile floor with an old towel.

Priya planted the tomato seed in the small pot. Warm sunlight reached the small pot every single morning. A green shoot rose from the soil inside the small pot. Priya moved the small pot to the sunny balcony.

The kettle boiled on the stove for several minutes. Steam rose from the kettle toward the white ceiling. The kettle whistled loudly across the quiet kitchen. Amara lifted the kettle off the stove with a cloth.
Lucas dropped the phone onto the concrete pavement outside. The screen of the phone cracked from corner to corner. The phone stopped responding to any touch. Lucas carried the phone to a repair shop.

Heavy snow fell on the mountain road all night. The mountain road disappeared under a thick white layer. Traffic on the mountain road stopped completely by morning. A plow cleared the mountain road before noon.
The bicycle tire rolled over a sharp piece of glass. The bicycle tire lost air within a few seconds. The bicycle became impossible to ride any further. Ravi patched the bicycle tire beside the road.
Sofia left the ice cream on the sunny table. The ice cream softened in the strong afternoon heat. The ice cream melted into a sticky puddle. Sofia washed the sunny table with soap.
The alarm clock rang at five in the morning. Nils woke immediately from a very deep sleep. Nils walked to the kitchen for strong coffee. Nils left the apartment before six in the morning.
A spark jumped from the campfire onto the dry grass. The dry grass caught fire within a few seconds. The fire spread across the dry grass toward the trees. Rangers put out the fire before midnight.
Mateo forgot to water the fern for three weeks. The fern lost color in every leaf. The fern dropped dry leaves onto the windowsill. Mateo threw the fern into the compost bin.

The river rose after four days of steady rain. The river covered the low fields near the village. Farmers moved the cattle to the higher ground above the village. The river returned to normal after a week.
Hannah stepped on the rusty nail in the old barn. The rusty nail cut deep into Hannah's foot. Hannah limped back toward the farmhouse in great pain. A doctor cleaned Hannah's foot in the evening.
The candle burned near the open window all evening. Wind pushed the curtain against the candle. The curtain caught fire at the lower edge. Peter smothered the fire with a heavy blanket.
Yusuf tripped over the loose carpet in the hallway. Yusuf fell hard against the wooden door. The wooden door swung open with a loud bang. Yusuf stood up slowly beside the wooden door.

The dog smelled bacon from the far end of the hallway. The dog jumped onto the kitchen counter with a single leap. The dog ate the entire plate of bacon in seconds. Ingrid found the empty plate at noon.

Clara studied the piano piece every evening for a month. Clara made fewer mistakes in the piano piece each week. Clara played the piano piece perfectly at the recital. The audience clapped for nearly a full minute.
The milk stood outside the refrigerator for two days. The milk turned sour in the summer heat. The milk smelled terrible near the open bottle. Omar poured the milk down the drain.

Heavy frost covered the windshield of the car during the night. The car refused to start in the cold morning air. Lena poured warm water slowly over the windshield. The car started after several more attempts.

Ahmed left the door of the birdcage open by mistake. The parrot flew out of the birdcage into the living room. The parrot landed on top of the tall bookshelf. Ahmed placed fresh seeds inside the birdcage.

The bread dough sat in the warm corner for two hours. The bread dough doubled in size inside the large bowl. Rosa shaped the bread dough into the round loaf. Rosa slid the round loaf into the hot oven.

A strong wind blew the newspaper off the wooden park bench. The newspaper landed in a wide muddy puddle nearby. The newspaper turned soft and completely unreadable within a minute. Kwame threw the newspaper into a public bin.
Nadia poured too much salt into the soup. The soup became far too salty to eat. Nadia added potatoes and water to the soup. The soup tasted much better after twenty minutes.

The battery of the flashlight ran out during the hike. The flashlight went dark in the middle of the forest. Ivan walked slowly along the path without light. Ivan reached the small cabin a little after midnight.

Marta forgot the umbrella at the office on Friday. Rain fell heavily during the walk to the station. Marta arrived at the station with completely wet clothes. Marta bought a new umbrella on Saturday morning.
The ice on the pond melted under the spring sun. The ice on the pond broke into floating pieces. Ducks swam across the open water of the pond. Children watched the pond from the wooden bridge.
Diego sanded the wooden chair for a full hour. Diego painted the wooden chair with blue paint. The wooden chair dried in the sun all afternoon. Diego placed the wooden chair on the porch.

The zipper of the backpack broke during the long trip. The books fell out of the backpack onto the wet street. Aisha collected the books from the wet street quickly. Aisha carried the backpack home in both arms.
The wasp flew into the classroom through the open window. Students jumped away from the desks in fear. The teacher opened the second window very wide. The wasp flew out into the garden.

Hiroshi forgot to lock the garden gate at night. The goat walked out of the garden gate before dawn. The goat ate the neighbor's flowers for an hour. Hiroshi led the goat back through the garden gate.
The water pipe cracked during the freezing night. Water spread across the bathroom floor by morning. Fatima closed the main valve in the basement. A plumber replaced the water pipe before noon.

Leo forgot the train ticket at home on the kitchen table. The train left the station exactly on time without Leo. Leo waited two hours for another train to the coast. Leo arrived at the meeting very late.

The oil in the frying pan became extremely hot. Bruno dropped a wet potato into the frying pan. The oil splashed out of the frying pan onto Bruno's arm. Bruno pressed a cold wet cloth against the burn.

The road crew closed the main street early on Monday. Cars moved to the narrow side streets around the center. Traffic in the center slowed to a crawl. Drivers reached the offices thirty minutes late.
Marisol filled the bathtub with very hot water. Marisol forgot the bathtub during a long phone call. Water ran over the edge of the bathtub. Marisol dried the bathroom floor with towels.
The chef added too much chili to the stew. The stew tasted painfully hot to the guests. The guests drank cold water throughout the entire meal. The chef removed the stew from the menu.

Snow melted on the mountain during the warm week. The stream below the mountain grew wide and fast. The stream carried the fallen branches toward the village bridge. Workers cleared the fallen branches from the village bridge.
Emil cut onions for the soup for ten minutes. Emil's eyes filled with tears in the small kitchen. Emil stopped cutting onions for a short break. Emil washed both hands with cold water.
The old bridge shook under the heavy truck. Cracks appeared along the concrete surface of the old bridge. Engineers closed the old bridge to all traffic. Repairs on the old bridge lasted six months.
Ana left the bicycle outside during the winter. Rust covered the chain of the bicycle by March. The chain of the bicycle snapped on the first ride. Ana bought a new chain at the shop.
The seagull grabbed the sandwich from Karl's hand. The seagull flew toward the roof of the wooden pier. Karl stood on the pier without any lunch. Karl bought a second sandwich at the kiosk.

A loud thunderclap frightened the horse in the open field. The horse jumped over the low wooden fence. The horse ran toward the dark forest at full speed. Farmers found the horse near the river at dusk.
Zoe forgot the password of the email account. The email account locked after five wrong attempts. Zoe called the support line for twenty minutes. Zoe entered the email account with a new password.

The waiter carried the plates across the crowded dining room. The waiter slipped on a wet spot near the bar. The plates crashed onto the hard tile floor. The waiter apologized to the guests at the table.

Steady rain filled the water barrel behind the house all week. The water barrel overflowed onto the narrow garden path. The garden path turned into soft brown mud. Jorge covered the garden path with fresh gravel.
The moth flew around the lamp for many minutes. The moth touched the hot glass of the lamp. The moth fell onto the wooden table. Selma carried the moth outside on a card.

Nathan lifted the heavy box without bending the knees. Nathan's back hurt badly on Sunday morning. Nathan visited a physiotherapist in the town center on Tuesday. Nathan lifted heavy boxes carefully after the injury.
The chocolate sat in the car during the hot afternoon. The chocolate melted inside the paper wrapper. The chocolate stained the fabric of the seat. Bea cleaned the seat with warm soapy water.

Wind pushed the sailboat far from the harbor. The sail of the sailboat tore near the top. The sailboat drifted slowly toward the rocky southern shore. A rescue boat towed the sailboat back to the harbor.

Farid planted the apple tree in the back garden. The apple tree grew taller every spring for ten years. The apple tree produced fruit in the eleventh summer. Farid picked apples from the apple tree in September.

The teacher wrote the assignment on the classroom whiteboard. Students copied the assignment into small paper notebooks. Students worked on the assignment for almost two weeks. The teacher collected the assignment on Friday morning.
Amina forgot to charge the laptop before the trip. The laptop ran out of power on the train. Amina wrote notes on paper for two hours. Amina charged the laptop in the hotel room after dinner.
The referee showed a red card to the striker. The striker left the field before halftime. The team played with ten players for an hour. The team lost the match by two goals.
Ice formed on the steps of the library overnight. Mr. Olsen slipped on the steps of the library. Mr. Olsen broke a wrist during the fall. A caretaker spread salt on the steps of the library.
Lucia read the recipe very quickly in the morning. Lucia used salt instead of sugar in the cake. The cake tasted strange to every guest. Lucia read the recipe carefully before the second attempt.

The tree roots grew under the concrete sidewalk for years. The concrete sidewalk cracked in several places. Pedestrians stumbled on the concrete sidewalk in the dark. The city replaced the concrete sidewalk in June.

A mouse chewed the cable behind the refrigerator. The refrigerator stopped cooling during the night. The food inside the refrigerator spoiled by morning. Petra called an electrician early on Saturday morning.

The volcano released the ash cloud into the sky on Monday. The ash cloud covered the fields around the volcano. Flights near the volcano stopped completely for a week. Farmers cleaned the fields for many weeks.

Karim watered the lawn every evening during the very dry summer. The lawn turned deep green within only two weeks. The lawn grew thick and tall by the middle of August. Karim cut the lawn with a small push mower.
A bee landed on Greta's arm during the long picnic. The bee stung Greta near the left elbow. Greta's arm swelled to twice the normal size. A pharmacist gave Greta a cooling cream.

Viktor knocked the wine glass across the white tablecloth. Red wine spread quickly through the white tablecloth. A dark stain appeared in the middle of the white tablecloth. Viktor washed the white tablecloth twice with hot water.
Freya pressed a sharp pin against the red balloon. The red balloon burst with a very loud bang. The children in the room screamed with surprise. Freya collected the pieces of the red balloon.
Tobias boiled the egg for twelve minutes in salty water. The egg cracked along one side inside the pot. White foam leaked from the egg into the water. Tobias ate the egg with bread and butter.

The children built the snowman in the front yard on Saturday. Warm sunshine reached the snowman on Sunday afternoon. The snowman shrank to a small white lump by Monday. The children found only a carrot on the grass.
Sanne flew the kite over the wide open field. The wind pushed the kite toward the tall poplar tree. The kite caught on a branch of the tall poplar tree. Sanne pulled the kite down with a long stick.

Bettina wrote the letter to the cousin in Chile. Bettina posted the letter at the small village office. The letter crossed the ocean over the following three weeks. The cousin in Chile read the letter in early June.

Old batteries stayed inside the television remote for many years. The old batteries leaked a white powder inside the television remote. The television remote stopped working during a rainy evening. Ida cleaned the television remote with a dry brush.

The paint can tipped over on the wooden ladder. Blue paint poured onto the gray carpet below the wooden ladder. The gray carpet absorbed the blue paint within seconds. Antonio replaced the gray carpet the following month.

The shoelace snapped during the second kilometer of the race. Pablo's shoe became loose on the rough gravel path. Pablo stopped beside the rough gravel path for nearly a minute. Pablo tied the shoelace into a short knot.

The goldfish jumped out of the round glass bowl. The goldfish landed on the wooden table near the window. The goldfish flapped hard for several long seconds. Nora returned the goldfish to the round glass bowl.

Emeka fell asleep on the beach in the strong midday sun. Emeka's shoulders turned bright red within two hours. Emeka's shoulders hurt badly for the rest of the week. Emeka bought sunscreen at the shop near the beach.

Wanda opened the chess game with the queen's pawn. Wanda captured the opponent's bishop on the twentieth move. Wanda attacked the opponent's king from two directions. Wanda won the chess game after fifty moves.
The baby cried loudly at three in the morning. Beatrice woke immediately in the dark bedroom. Beatrice warmed a bottle of milk in the kitchen. The baby slept quietly again before four.

The butter sat on the counter through the hot afternoon. The butter softened into a shiny yellow pool. The butter dripped over the edge of the small dish. Sven put the butter back in the refrigerator.

The storm knocked down a power line outside the village. Every house in the village lost electricity before dinner. Families in the village lit candles in the kitchens. A repair crew fixed the power line at dawn.

Sugar spilled under the kitchen window on Tuesday morning. Ants found the sugar under the kitchen window on Wednesday. A long line of ants crossed the kitchen floor by Thursday. Dolores cleaned the kitchen floor with vinegar on Friday.

The coffee cup tipped over on the office desk. Hot coffee soaked the front of Reza's white shirt. A brown stain covered the front of Reza's white shirt. Reza changed into a spare shirt from the drawer.

A small bird flew toward the large window of the museum. The small bird hit the large window with a soft thud. The small bird lay stunned on the stone terrace. A guard carried the small bird to the garden.

A nail lay in the middle of the country road. The truck tire rolled over the nail near the bridge. The truck tire lost air over the following ten kilometers. The driver changed the truck tire beside the country road.

Bruno carried old boards across the yard without gloves. A splinter entered Bruno's thumb near the nail. Bruno's thumb turned red and sore by evening. A neighbor removed the splinter with small tweezers.
Célia watered the cactus twice every week all winter. The soil around the cactus stayed wet for months. The cactus turned soft and brown at the base. Célia moved the cactus to a pot with dry sand.

The keys slipped through Deniz's fingers near the street corner. The keys fell through the metal grate into the drain. Deniz stared into the drain for a long minute. A worker lifted the keys out with a magnet.

Franka filled the ice cube tray with water at noon. The ice cube tray sat in the freezer for four hours. The water in the ice cube tray turned to solid ice. Franka dropped two ice cubes into a glass of juice.
Rain fell on the open book on the garden bench. The pages of the open book swelled with water. The pages of the open book stuck together completely. Malik dried the open book near a warm radiator.

Yuki mixed flour and water in a small glass jar. The mixture in the small glass jar bubbled after three days. A sour smell rose from the small glass jar on the fourth day. Yuki baked the first sourdough loaf on Sunday.

The garden gate hinge collected rust over three wet winters. The garden gate squealed loudly with every single push. The garden gate stuck halfway open in March. Ottó dripped oil onto the garden gate hinge.

Pepper dust rose from the grinder over the salad bowl. The pepper dust reached Camille's nose within a second. Camille sneezed five times beside the salad bowl. Camille closed the grinder tightly after the meal.

The cat disappeared from the apartment on a cold Friday. Neighbors searched the courtyard and the cellar all weekend. The cat appeared on the roof of the bicycle shed on Monday. Rafael carried the cat back into the apartment.

The tall candle burned on the dining table for six hours. Hot wax ran down the side of the tall candle. Hot wax hardened on the dining table in white drops. Aurora scraped the wax off the dining table with a knife.

The phone charger bent sharply behind the heavy bed. The wire inside the phone charger broke near the plug. The phone charger stopped delivering power on Thursday. Kenji bought a new phone charger at the mall.

The neighbor's dog barked at cars from midnight until dawn. Adela lay awake in the bedroom for five hours. Adela fell asleep at work during the morning meeting. Adela spoke with the neighbor about the dog on Saturday.

Isabel tuned the guitar string too tightly before the concert. The guitar string snapped with a sharp metallic sound. The guitar string cut a thin line across Isabel's finger. Isabel replaced the guitar string during the break.

The teacher wrote long formulas on the old chalkboard. Chalk dust floated through the air near the old chalkboard. Chalk dust covered the front desks by the end of class. A cleaner wiped the front desks after school.
Halvor poured boiling water over the tea leaves at eight. The tea leaves stayed in the pot for twenty minutes. The tea turned dark and very bitter. Halvor drank the tea with three spoons of honey.

The camera slipped from the boat into the shallow lake. Water entered the camera through the battery door. The camera stopped working within a few seconds. Yara placed the camera in a bag of dry rice.

The wooden fence stood in wet soil for fifteen years. The posts of the wooden fence rotted below the ground. The wooden fence leaned toward the neighbor's driveway. Gustav rebuilt the wooden fence with concrete posts.

Naomi ran five kilometers every morning from January until April. Naomi's legs grew stronger with each passing week. Naomi ran the city marathon in early May. Naomi rested on the sofa for two full days.
noncausal_texts: A violin is played with a bow. A flute is played by blowing air. A drum is played by striking the drumhead. A harp is played by plucking strings.

An oak tree produces acorns during the autumn season. A birch tree carries thin white bark around the trunk. A willow tree grows well in very wet soil. A pine tree keeps green needles throughout the entire winter.

Copper conducts electricity very well in household wiring. Iron rusts in the presence of water and oxygen. Aluminium weighs very little for a given volume. Mercury remains liquid at ordinary room temperature.

A penguin swims well but cannot fly at all. An owl hunts small animals during the dark hours. A woodpecker drills holes in tree trunks with a strong beak. A hummingbird feeds on nectar from bright flowers.

Lisbon sits on the Atlantic coast as the capital of Portugal. Nairobi serves as the capital city of Kenya in East Africa. Ottawa functions as the capital city of Canada. Canberra holds the role of capital city in Australia.
Portuguese is the official language of Brazil. Arabic is the official language of Egypt. Japanese is the main language spoken across Japan. Swahili is widely spoken in Tanzania and Kenya.

A tennis racket has strings stretched across an oval frame. A hockey stick has a curved blade at the lower end. A golf club has a metal head on a long shaft. A baseball bat is a smooth wooden or metal cylinder.

A refrigerator keeps food cold with a mechanical cooling system. A blender chops soft food with fast spinning blades. A toaster browns bread with hot metal heating elements. An electric kettle heats water to the boiling point.

A bee collects nectar from flowers during warm weather. A mosquito feeds on blood through a thin sharp tube. An ant lives in a colony with thousands of other ants. A butterfly tastes food through the feet.

The heart pumps blood through the entire human body. The lungs exchange oxygen and carbon dioxide with the air. The liver removes many harmful substances from the blood. The kidneys filter waste products out of the bloodstream.

Cotton comes from the fluffy fibers around cotton seeds. Wool comes from the fleece of sheep and certain goats. Silk comes from the cocoons of silkworms. Linen comes from the stalks of the flax plant.

A lemon tastes sour because of a high acid content. A banana grows on a tall plant in tropical regions. A strawberry carries small seeds on the outer surface. A coconut contains liquid inside a hard brown shell.

Fog forms when water droplets hang close to the ground. Hail falls as small balls of ice from storm clouds. Lightning is a very large electrical discharge in the sky. A rainbow appears when sunlight passes through water droplets.

A tram runs on rails through many European city streets. A ferry carries passengers and cars across open water. A helicopter lifts straight upward with spinning rotor blades. A submarine travels below the surface of the ocean.

The yen serves as the official currency throughout Japan. The rupee is the official currency used across India. The peso is the official currency of Mexico. The won is used as the official currency in South Korea.

A ruby shows a deep red color in natural light. An emerald shows a rich green color in natural light. A sapphire commonly appears in a deep blue shade. A diamond is the hardest natural material on earth.

An octopus has eight arms covered with many strong suckers. A jellyfish drifts through the ocean without any bones. A dolphin breathes air through a blowhole on the back. A shark grows new teeth throughout the entire life.

Feta comes from Greece and rests in salty brine. Gouda comes from the Netherlands and has a firm texture. Brie comes from France and carries a soft white rind. Parmesan comes from Italy and has a hard dry body.
Baguette is a long thin loaf associated with France. Naan is a flat soft bread from South Asia. Pumpernickel is a dark heavy rye bread from Germany. Focaccia is a flat oiled bread from Italy.

The tango developed in the neighborhoods of Buenos Aires. Flamenco developed in the southern region of Spain. The waltz is danced in three beats per measure. Samba is danced with quick steps and strong hip movement.

A hammer drives nails into wood with a heavy head. A saw cuts through wood with a row of sharp teeth. A screwdriver turns screws with a shaped metal tip. A spirit level shows a flat surface with a bubble in liquid.

Granite is an igneous rock with visible crystal grains. Limestone is a sedimentary rock made mostly of calcium carbonate. Marble is a metamorphic rock formed from limestone under pressure. Basalt is a dark igneous rock formed from cooled lava.

Cumulus clouds appear as white puffy shapes in fair weather. Cirrus clouds appear as thin wispy lines very high up. Stratus clouds cover the sky as a flat gray layer. Cumulonimbus clouds bring thunderstorms and heavy rain.

A chameleon changes skin color for camouflage and communication. A crocodile lives in rivers and swamps in warm regions. A gecko climbs smooth walls with special pads on the toes. A tortoise carries a hard protective shell on the back.

A sunflower turns a large yellow head toward bright light. A tulip grows from a bulb planted in the soil. A rose carries sharp thorns along the green stem. A lavender plant produces a strong scent from purple flowers.

Cinnamon comes from the inner bark of a tropical tree. Saffron comes from the dried threads inside a purple crocus. Black pepper comes from the dried berries of a climbing vine. Ginger comes from an underground stem with a sharp flavor.

Oxygen makes up about one fifth of the atmosphere of earth. Helium is a very light gas used inside floating balloons. Carbon forms the basis of all known living organisms. Neon glows bright orange inside electric display tubes.

The Sahara covers a large part of northern Africa. The Atacama in Chile receives almost no rain at all. The Gobi stretches across Mongolia and northern China. The Mojave lies in the southwestern United States.

The Nile flows through northeastern Africa toward the Mediterranean Sea. The Amazon carries an enormous volume of water through South America. The Danube passes through many countries in central Europe. The Mekong flows through Southeast Asia toward the sea.

Mount Fuji is a volcano on the Japanese island of Honshu. Kilimanjaro rises above the plains of northern Tanzania. The Matterhorn stands on the border between Switzerland and Italy. Denali is located in the state of Alaska.

A hexagon has six sides of equal length in the regular form. A circle has every point at the same distance from the center. A triangle has three sides and three interior angles. A cube has six square faces and twelve edges.

A meter measures length in the international system of units. A kilogram measures mass in the international system of units. A second measures time in the international system of units. An ampere measures electric current in the international system of units.

Chess is played on a board with sixty four squares. Go is played with black and white stones on a grid. Backgammon is played with dice and round checkers. Scrabble is played with letter tiles on a word grid.

A button mushroom grows widely on commercial farms. A truffle grows underground near the roots of certain trees. Yeast is a fungus used in bread and beer production. Penicillium mold produced the first widely used antibiotic.
A cow produces milk after the birth of a calf. A hen lays eggs in a nest inside the coop. A sheep grows a thick wool coat during the cold months. A goat eats a very wide range of rough plants.

An almond grows inside a fruit related to the peach. A cashew grows attached to the bottom of a fleshy fruit. A walnut has a wrinkled kernel inside a hard shell. A pistachio splits open along the shell when ripe.

Reggae developed in Jamaica with a strong offbeat rhythm. Jazz developed in New Orleans with improvised solo passages. Fado developed in Portugal with melancholy vocal lines. Blues developed in the American South with a twelve bar structure.
A cardiologist treats problems of the human heart. A dermatologist treats conditions of the skin. A pediatrician treats infants and young children. A radiologist reads medical images for diagnosis.

Orion contains a line of three bright stars in a row. Cassiopeia forms a wide shape similar to the letter W. The Southern Cross appears in the skies of the southern hemisphere. Ursa Major contains the group known as the Big Dipper.

Lake Baikal in Siberia holds an enormous volume of fresh water. Lake Victoria borders Uganda and Tanzania and Kenya. The Dead Sea contains water with a very high salt content. Lake Titicaca lies high in the Andes between Peru and Bolivia.

Basil grows well in warm sunny conditions with regular water. Mint spreads quickly through underground runners in a garden. Rosemary keeps needle shaped leaves throughout the whole year. Parsley produces flat or curled green leaves.

Saturn is surrounded by wide rings of ice and rock. Mars appears red because of iron oxide across the surface. Venus has a thick atmosphere of carbon dioxide. Jupiter has a large storm known as the Great Red Spot.

The knight moves in an L shape across the chessboard. The bishop moves along diagonal lines of a single color. The rook moves in straight lines along ranks and files. The pawn moves forward but captures on the diagonal.

A wide aperture produces a shallow depth of field. A fast shutter speed freezes rapid motion in a photograph. A high ISO setting adds visible noise to a photograph. A tripod holds a camera steady during long exposures.

A soccer match consists of two halves of forty five minutes. A basketball hoop stands three point zero five meters above the floor. A tennis set is won with at least six games. A marathon covers forty two point two kilometers.

Balsa wood is very light and used in model building. Oak wood is dense and common in furniture construction. Cedar wood carries a strong scent that repels some insects. Bamboo grows extremely fast and works well as building material.

A honeybee colony contains a single queen and many workers. Worker bees build honeycomb from wax produced in body glands. A drone bee has no sting at all. Honeybees communicate the location of flowers through a waggle dance.

Mount Etna sits on the island of Sicily in Italy. Krakatoa erupted violently in the Sunda Strait of Indonesia. Mauna Loa forms a large part of the island of Hawaii. Popocatépetl rises near Mexico City in central Mexico.

Antarctica holds the majority of the fresh water on earth. Penguins live in the southern hemisphere rather than the Arctic. Polar bears hunt seals across the sea ice of the Arctic. The Arctic Ocean is the smallest ocean on the planet.

The Eiffel Tower is built from wrought iron in Paris. The Colosseum in Rome was built from stone and concrete. The Taj Mahal in Agra is faced with white marble. The Sydney Opera House has a roof of white shell shapes.

Arabica beans grow best at high altitude in tropical regions. Espresso is made by forcing hot water through fine coffee grounds. Decaffeinated coffee has most of the caffeine removed before roasting. Cold brew coffee is steeped in cold water for many hours.

Green tea leaves are heated quickly to prevent oxidation. Black tea leaves are fully oxidized before drying. Oolong tea leaves are partly oxidized during processing. Herbal infusions contain no leaves from the tea plant.

A road bicycle has thin tires and drop handlebars. A mountain bicycle has wide knobby tires and strong suspension. A tandem bicycle carries two riders on a single frame. A folding bicycle collapses for storage in small spaces.

Letterpress printing presses raised type directly onto paper. Screen printing pushes ink through a fine mesh stencil. Offset printing transfers ink from a plate to a rubber blanket. Inkjet printing sprays tiny droplets of ink onto paper.

A bowline forms a loop that does not slip under load. A reef knot joins two ropes of similar thickness. A clove hitch attaches a rope to a post or rail. A figure eight knot stops a rope from running through a ring.

A prime number has exactly two distinct positive divisors. An even number is divisible by two without a remainder. An irrational number cannot be written as a simple fraction. A negative number lies to the left of zero on the number line.

RAM stores data temporarily while a computer is running. A hard drive stores data even without any electrical power. A CPU performs the arithmetic and logic operations of a computer. A GPU handles many parallel calculations for graphics and other tasks.

Python uses indentation to define blocks of code. JavaScript runs inside web browsers on nearly every website. SQL is used for queries against relational databases. C is a compiled language used for operating systems.

The Cyrillic alphabet is used for Russian and several other languages. Hangul was created for the Korean language in the fifteenth century. Arabic script is written from right to left. Chinese characters represent meaning rather than individual sounds.

Red light has a longer wavelength than blue light. Mixing yellow and blue paint produces a green shade. White light contains every color of the visible spectrum. Black surfaces absorb most of the light that arrives.

The retina at the back of the eye detects light. The cochlea inside the ear converts vibrations into nerve signals. Taste buds on the tongue detect sweet and bitter compounds. The skin contains receptors for pressure and temperature.

Tyrannosaurus rex walked on two powerful hind legs. Triceratops carried three horns on a large bony frill. Stegosaurus had rows of tall plates along the back. Velociraptor was roughly the size of a large turkey.

The Egyptians built pyramids as tombs for the pharaohs. The Romans constructed aqueducts to carry water into cities. The Maya developed a detailed calendar system in Central America. The Inca built roads through the mountains of South America.

The printing press with movable type spread rapidly through Europe. The telephone transmits the human voice over long distances. A compass points toward the magnetic north of the earth. A microscope reveals structures too small for the human eye.

Marie Curie conducted pioneering research on radioactivity. Charles Darwin described natural selection in the theory of evolution. Isaac Newton formulated the laws of motion and universal gravitation. Alan Turing developed foundational ideas in computer science.

Vaccines train the immune system against specific pathogens. Antibiotics act against bacteria rather than viruses. Insulin regulates the level of glucose in the blood. Anesthesia blocks the sensation of pain during surgery.
A light year measures distance rather than time. A black hole has gravity strong enough to trap light. A comet develops a bright tail near the sun. A nebula is a large cloud of gas and dust in space.

An earthquake releases energy along a fault in the crust. A glacier moves slowly under the weight of accumulated ice. A geyser sends hot water upward from underground chambers. A canyon is carved by a river over a long period.

A coral reef is built by tiny animals called polyps. A tsunami is caused by the sudden displacement of ocean water. Tides result from the gravitational pull of the moon and sun. Ocean water contains roughly thirty five grams of salt per liter.

Arctic terns migrate between the Arctic and the Antarctic. Swallows build nests from mud under roofs and bridges. Flamingos obtain a pink color from pigments in food. Ostriches run faster than any other bird on land.

A greyhound reaches very high speed over a short distance. A dachshund was bred for hunting badgers in narrow burrows. A border collie is used for herding sheep on farms. A siberian husky pulls sleds across snow in cold regions.

Braising combines browning with slow cooking in liquid. Steaming cooks food with vapor rather than direct water contact. Grilling cooks food over direct heat from below or above. Poaching cooks food gently in liquid below the boiling point.

Champagne comes from a specific region in northern France. Riesling is a white grape common in German vineyards. Tannins in red wine come mainly from grape skins and seeds. Port is a fortified wine from the Douro valley in Portugal.

A dome distributes weight evenly around a circular base. A flying buttress supports a wall from the outside. A column transfers weight from a roof down to the ground. An arch spans an opening with wedge shaped stones.

Oil paint dries slowly and allows extensive blending. Watercolor paint relies on the white of the paper for light areas. Acrylic paint dries quickly and becomes water resistant. Fresco painting is applied onto wet plaster on a wall.

A soliloquy presents the thoughts of a character aloud on stage. A proscenium arch frames the stage in many traditional theaters. An understudy learns a role for possible emergency performance. A prompter supplies forgotten lines to actors during a performance.

A close up shot fills the frame with a face or object. A tracking shot follows movement with a camera on rails. A montage compresses time through a series of short shots. A voice over adds narration outside the visible action.

A haiku follows a pattern of three short lines. A sonnet contains fourteen lines in a fixed rhyme scheme. Alliteration repeats initial consonant sounds across nearby words. Iambic pentameter contains five metrical feet per line.
A noun names a person or a place or a thing. A verb expresses an action or a state. An adjective describes a quality of a noun. An adverb modifies a verb or an adjective.

A contract requires an offer and an acceptance and consideration. A jury decides questions of fact in certain trials. A subpoena compels a witness to appear in court. Precedent guides courts through decisions in earlier similar cases.

Inflation describes a general rise in the price level. A tariff is a tax applied to imported goods. Supply and demand interact to set prices in a market. GDP measures the total value of goods and services produced.

A bond represents a loan made to a government or company. A share represents partial ownership of a company. Interest is the cost of borrowing money over time. A dividend is a payment made to shareholders from profits.

Water consists of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom. Table salt consists of sodium and chlorine in a crystal lattice. An acid releases hydrogen ions in a water solution. A catalyst speeds a reaction without being consumed.

Sound travels faster through steel than through air. Light travels at roughly three hundred thousand kilometers per second. Friction converts kinetic energy into heat. Gravity attracts every mass toward every other mass.

DNA carries genetic information in a double helix structure. A cell membrane controls the passage of substances into a cell. Photosynthesis converts light energy into chemical energy in plants. Mitochondria produce energy inside most animal and plant cells.

An isthmus is a narrow strip of land between two seas. A fjord is a deep coastal inlet carved by glacial action. An archipelago is a group of islands in a body of water. A delta forms where a river deposits sediment at the mouth.

A roundabout keeps traffic moving in a single direction around a center. A cable car is pulled by a moving cable on a slope. A funicular uses two counterbalanced cars on a steep track. A monorail runs on a single elevated rail.

Denim is a sturdy cotton fabric with a diagonal weave. A kimono is a traditional garment worn in Japan. A beret is a soft round cap without a brim. Tweed is a rough woolen cloth associated with Scotland.

Cricket is played with a bat and a ball and wickets. Rugby allows passing the ball backward rather than forward. Judo originated in Japan as a form of unarmed combat. Fencing uses three weapons with different rules.

The Olympic flame is lit in Olympia in Greece. The marathon commemorates a run described in ancient Greek history. The Paralympic Games feature athletes with physical impairments. The Olympic rings represent five inhabited continents.

Wheat is harvested with a machine called a combine. Rice grows in flooded fields across many parts of Asia. Coffee grows on shrubs in tropical highland regions. Grapes are grown in vineyards for wine and fruit.

The baobab stores large amounts of water inside a thick trunk. The redwood grows to great height along the California coast. The mangrove grows in salt water along tropical coastlines. The ginkgo has existed in a similar form for millions of years.

Glass is made by melting sand at very high temperature. Concrete is made from cement and water and aggregate. Paper is made from wood fibers pressed into thin sheets. Rubber comes from the latex of certain tropical trees.

A solar panel converts sunlight into electrical current. A wind turbine converts moving air into rotational energy. A hydroelectric dam uses falling water to spin turbines. A nuclear reactor releases energy through the splitting of atoms.

The Apollo program landed astronauts on the moon. The Hubble telescope orbits above the atmosphere of the earth. The Voyager probes carry a golden record with sounds from earth. The International Space Station orbits a few hundred kilometers above the surface.

Morse code represents letters with short and long signals. Braille represents letters with raised dots for reading by touch. Sign language uses hand shapes and facial expression for meaning. Semaphore uses flag positions to signal letters across a distance.

A leap year adds an extra day to the month of February. A lunar month lasts roughly twenty nine and a half days. The Gregorian calendar is used for civil purposes in most countries. A sundial indicates time through the position of a shadow.

Freezing slows the growth of microorganisms in food. Pickling preserves food in an acidic liquid. Smoking preserves food with compounds from burning wood. Drying removes the water needed by spoilage organisms.

A food chain describes the transfer of energy between organisms. Decomposers break down dead material into simpler substances. A keystone species has a large effect on an ecosystem. Biodiversity describes the variety of life in a given area.

REM sleep is associated with vivid dreaming. Melatonin is a hormone connected to the sleep cycle. Circadian rhythms follow a cycle of roughly twenty four hours. Caffeine blocks receptors involved in the feeling of sleepiness.
